items.title: Gold Steadies as Hopes of US-Iran Truce Lower Odds of Rate Hikes
items.summary: Gold was little changed as optimism over efforts to end the Middle East conflict eased bets on interest-rate hikes.

items.title: Oil Edges Up After Plunging on Optimism Over US-Iran Agreement
items.summary: Oil inched higher after plummeting on Wednesday, as President Donald Trump said the US is in the “final stages” with Iran.

items.title: Bound for Mars, Elon Musk's SpaceX unveils filing for blockbuster IPO
items.summary: SpaceX has filed for its initial public offering. The company aims for a trillion-dollar market debut. This listing could set a precedent for future IPOs from major tech firms. SpaceX's valuation could reach $1.75 trillion.

items.title: US Treasuries Rebound on Optimism for US-Iran Deal Progress
items.summary: Treasuries surged on investor optimism that the US and Iran were working toward a deal that could ease a rise in global energy prices that has fanned fears around inflation.

items.title: Nvidia posts record $81.6 billion quarterly revenue on AI spending boom
items.summary: Chip giant Nvidia on Wednesday posted record quarterly revenue of $81.6 billion, blowing past Wall Street forecasts as insatiable demand for its artificial intelligence hardware powered another blockbuster quarter. The results for the first quarter of fiscal 2027, ending April 26, marked an 85 percent jump from the same period a year ago and a 20 percent rise from the prior quarter, underscoring Nvidia's status as the primary beneficiary of a global AI infrastructure buildout. arp/sms

items.title: Nvidia posts record $81.6 billion quarterly revenue on AI spending boom
items.summary: Chip giant Nvidia on Wednesday posted record quarterly revenue of $81.6 billion, blowing past Wall Street forecasts as insatiable demand for its artificial intelligence hardware powered another blockbuster quarter.

items.title: US stocks today: Dow soars 600 pts, Nasdaq 1.5% as chip stocks rally eyeing Nvidia results, Iran peace deal hopes
items.summary: Wall Street indexes jumped over 1% led by tech and semiconductor rally ahead of Nvidia earnings. Target fell on weak outlook. Airlines gained as oil dropped. Fed minutes signaled possible rate hike debate. Nasdaq S&P Dow Jones rose strongly as AI optimism offset inflation and geopolitical concerns sentiment rises

items.title: Gold loans rise 50% to Rs 19 lakh crore in FY26
items.summary: Gold jewellery loans have seen a massive 50 percent jump year-on-year, reaching ₹19 lakh crore. This makes it the fastest growing retail loan category. The surge is driven by rising gold prices and a shift in borrowing habits. This growth has boosted the overall retail loan market. Asset quality has also improved with declining delinquency levels across most segments.

items.title: Nods for Rs 30,000 crore shipbuilding cluster in Andhra Pradesh this month: Minister Sonowal
items.summary: India plans a major shipbuilding cluster in Andhra Pradesh with ₹30,000 crore investment. This move aims to boost the Indian fleet size. Projects for West Bengal's waterways and ports are also set to accelerate. India has repatriated over 3,000 seafarers due to West Asia disruptions. Shipping services to alternate ports have significantly increased.

items.title: Nayara, Shell cede market share in April; RIL-BP gains ground
items.summary: Private fuel sellers Nayara Energy and Shell saw sales drop significantly in April. They increased prices after the Iran war began. Consumers shifted to state-run companies that kept prices steady. This caused market share losses for Nayara and Shell. RIL-BP however reported strong sales growth. Localized fuel shortages occurred as demand moved to neighboring stations.

items.title: CBIC allows remote clearance for sea cargo
items.summary: The Central Board of Indirect Taxes and Customs has introduced new procedures for vessel entry and departure. Customs officers will now grant clearance remotely, eliminating the need for physical boarding. This change aims to accelerate port operations and reduce delays across India. Mandatory online filing of cargo declarations will enable this faceless and paperless system.

items.title: West Asia conflict fails to slow oil PSUs' capex push
items.summary: Indian oil companies are pushing ahead with major expansion and clean energy projects. Indian Oil Corporation plans significant investments in refining and renewable energy. Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited is also allocating substantial funds for refining and marketing. Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited's capital expenditure is expected to be slightly lower. These companies are focused on growth and energy transition.

items.title: CCI to onboard analysts and data scientists
items.summary: The Competition Commission of India is enhancing its capabilities to understand digital markets and artificial intelligence. The regulator is hiring data scientists and analysts with expertise in AI tools and digital market operations. This move aims to strengthen the CCI's oversight. The CCI is also increasing approvals for non-problematic transactions through an automated route.

items.title: Lower user development fee okayed for Navi Mumbai Airport
items.summary: Navi Mumbai International Airport will see lower passenger charges. India's airport tariff regulator has approved reduced user development fees. Domestic flyers will pay ₹620 and international flyers ₹1,225. This decision moderates charges at the new airport. The regulator also cut the airport's revenue requirement significantly. These changes will impact revenue recovery for the operator.

items.title: As India's R&D faces funding & talent crunch, Niti panel suggests 50+ fixes
items.summary: India's research and development spending lags behind global leaders. A Niti Aayog report highlights funding issues and bureaucratic delays. The report suggests increasing R&D investment to 2% of GDP. It also calls for streamlining processes and boosting manpower. Reforms aim to enhance India's research landscape and competitiveness.

items.title: TCS CEO Krithivasan earns over  ₹28 crore in FY26, still behind top IT CEOs: Here’s what his peers got
items.summary: TCS CEO K Krithivasan earned over  ₹28 crore in FY26, up 6.3%, driven mainly by performance-linked pay. His compensation is about 333 times the median employee salary. Compared to peers, he ranks mid-tier among IT CEOs, with industry-wide executive pay and inequality continuing to rise.

items.title: Fewer top-ups mark FMCG buying shift
items.summary: Indian shoppers are changing their habits. They are visiting stores less often for everyday items. However, when they do shop, they are spending more money each time. This shows a move away from quick buys towards planned purchases. Categories like biscuits and snacks are now part of deliberate shopping lists. This reflects a more cautious consumer approach.

items.title: Warehousing beats all odds amid global supply chaos
items.summary: India's warehousing sector shows strong growth in the first quarter of 2026. Leasing activity increased by 15 percent compared to the previous year, reaching 19.3 million square feet across eight major cities. This surge is fueled by demand from manufacturing and third-party logistics firms. Companies are actively enhancing their supply chain networks and distribution capabilities.

items.title: Ban on sale of alcohol in tetra packs? SC seeks Centre's view
items.summary: The Supreme Court has flagged alcohol sold in tetra packs and sachets as deceptive. Notices have been issued to the government and manufacturers like Globus Spirits and Wave Distilleries. The court is concerned about inconspicuous packaging that may resemble fruit juices. This packaging lacks clear warnings and potentially targets underage consumers. The next hearing is scheduled for August 10.

items.title: Whey to protein-rich foods gets costlier on global supply crunch
items.summary: India's growing demand for protein products is clashing with a global shortage of raw whey. This key ingredient's price has surged dramatically, forcing Indian brands to increase prices. Companies are absorbing some costs to protect consumers. The rising expense of protein powders and snacks is making daily protein goals more costly for many.

items.title: Zee always open to unlocking value in music business, says CEO Punit Goenka
items.summary: Zee Entertainment Enterprises is exploring ways to boost its music business, Zee Music Company. Options include separating it into a new company or bringing in a strategic partner. This move comes as the company focuses on growth areas like OTT and live events. Zee Music Company is a growing asset with significant subscriber numbers and revenue potential.

items.title: Dollar dips on hopes for Iran deal, yen near danger zone
items.summary: GLOBAL-FOREX:Dollar dips on hopes for Iran deal, yen near danger zone

items.title: Chicago Zillow Listings Disappear After Local Group Yanks Data
items.summary: In Chicago, scrolling Zillow for dream homes or apartments may soon be a thing of the past.

items.title: Q-comm sales for FMCG giants double this year
items.summary: Quick commerce now represents 6% of FMCG sales, doubling from FY25, with Dabur and Britannia leading at 9% of total sales. This channel drives a significant majority of online sales for these companies, accounting for 75% and 70% respectively. Growth is linked to premium brands and impulse purchases, though Hindustan Unilever sees demand for daily essentials.

items.title: RPSG, Inox, Hexa bid for Enel's renewable energy projects in India
items.summary: The three firms submitted their bids last week in the ₹3,000 crore enterprise value range for Enel's India business comprising renewable energy generation projects with a potential capacity of 2.5 gigawatts, the people said. The bids were preceded by a six-week period of due diligence during which the Indian suitors were given access to the assets of Enel Green Power India Pvt Ltd.

items.title: Apollo Hospitals FY26 profit jumps 34% to Rs 1,942 crore on growth across core businesses
items.summary: Apollo Hospitals announced robust financial results for the fourth quarter and the full fiscal year 2026. Consolidated revenue and net profit saw significant year-on-year increases. Growth was fueled by its healthcare services, diagnostics, and retail health segments. The company is expanding its bed capacity and focusing on underserved markets.

items.title: Tepid demand for US Treasury auction shows investor jitters over Iran war
items.summary: USA-BONDS/AUCTION:Tepid demand for US Treasury auction shows investor jitters over Iran war

items.title: India's peak power demand hits record 265.4 GW amid heatwave conditions
items.summary: The latest peak surpassed Tuesday's all-time high of 260.5 GW recorded during solar hours. The latest demand levels have already crossed last summer's record peak of 243 GW, and even those recorded during the evening. The peak demand recorded is inching closer to the projected 271 GW for the season.

items.title: OpenAI aiming for speedy IPO, source says, as market awaits SpaceX filing
items.summary: OPENAI-IPO:OpenAI aiming for speedy IPO, source says, as market awaits SpaceX filing

items.title: One-year forward rupee rate breaches 100 per US dollar mark
items.summary: The Indian rupee has crossed the Rs 100 per US dollar mark in its one-year forward rate. This comes as the spot market saw the rupee hit another historic low. Unabated dollar outflows and elevated crude prices are fueling the currency's weakness. Market watchers believe the pace of depreciation could slow if geopolitical tensions ease.

items.title: Fed minutes show more policymakers were prepared to lay groundwork for rate hike
items.summary: Federal Reserve minutes show growing concern that inflation driven by the Iran war could persist above the 2% target, prompting discussions around possible future rate hikes. Several policymakers signalled tightening may be needed, marking a more hawkish shift. Bond yields rose as markets priced in reduced chances of rate cuts.

items.title: AI use cases may lead to Rs 100 crore savings for Air India
items.summary: Air India is leveraging AI extensively, developing over 30 in-house tools to save approximately Rs 100 crore annually. These AI applications range from customer service, reducing call volumes by 50%, to complex operational tasks like predictive maintenance and optimizing on-time performance across its fleet. This digital overhaul aims to enhance efficiency and address the airline's accumulated losses.

items.title: Atal Pension Scheme not to be limited to Rs 5,000 monthly benefit? Here's what PFRDA chairman says
items.summary: The PFRDA Chairman has said that the current minimum fixed pension allowed under the Atal Pension scheme is inadequate.

items.title: Cloudnine to acquire Apollo’s maternity chain Cradle
items.summary: Cloudnine Hospitals is set to acquire Apollo Cradle and Apollo Fertility. This strategic move will create India's largest maternity and children's healthcare network. Apollo Health and Lifestyle Limited will hold a significant stake in the combined entity. The deal aims to enhance specialized healthcare access across India.

items.title: “Melody” meets Dalal Street: Parle Industries hits upper circuit on viral name confusion trade
items.summary: Parle Industries Limited hit the upper circuit, surging 5 per cent to Rs 5.25 after a viral “Melody” exchange between PM Narendra Modi and Italian PM Giorgia Meloni sparked retail frenzy, triggering speculative buying driven by name confusion rather than fundamentals.

items.title: Apollo Hospitals bets on expansion, clinical edge to maintain lead
items.summary: Apollo Hospitals plans to add 1,500 beds in the next 12-18 months to strengthen its lead in India’s private healthcare market. The company reported strong FY26 growth across hospitals, diagnostics and pharmacy businesses, aided by expansion and clinical specialization.

items.title: Bharat Petroleum hikes Russia’s share in crude oil purchases to 41% in bid to fill supply gaps
items.summary: BPCL has increased the share of Russian crude from 31% in the fourth quarter of the previous financial year to 41% at present

items.title: OpenAI preparing to file for IPO in coming days or weeks, WSJ reports
items.summary: OpenAI is reportedly preparing to file for an IPO in the coming days or weeks, according to a Wall Street Journal report citing sources. The ChatGPT maker is working with Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley on a confidential draft prospectus. The company has not officially confirmed the development.

items.title: Melody to ears? India's toffee exports are on a roll
items.summary: Exports of toffees and similar confectionery products grew over 10% in volume and 166% in rupee terms since FY14, reaching as many as 74 countries during the period.

items.title: Dr. Reddy’s launches Semaglutide tablets in India
items.summary: Semaglutide is the active ingredient in Danish major Novo Nordisk’s blockbuster diabetes and c, Wegovy and Ozempic

items.title: Lincoln International valued at $2.3 billion as shares jump in NYSE debut
items.summary: Lincoln International shares surged 12.6% on their New York Stock Exchange debut. The investment bank is now valued at approximately $2.3 billion. The Chicago-based firm's stock opened above its offer price. Lincoln and selling stockholders raised $421 million through the initial public offering. Investment bank IPOs have been infrequent in recent years.

items.title: Southwest Airlines to expand India global centre to 1,000 employees, executive says
items.summary: The U.S. airline, which ‌opened its Global ⁠Innovation ⁠Center in India's Hyderabad city on Wednesday, has already hired more than a dozen ​employees and intends to scale up to 200 employees in the near term, ​Krishna Kallepalli, Vice President and Global Head of Innovation (India) at Southwest Airlines, said in an interview.

items.title: India’s core sector growth edges up to 1.7% in April on steel, cement boost
items.summary: Steel production rose 6.2% year-on-year in April, cement output increased 9.4%, while electricity generation expanded 4.1%, supporting the overall index growth.

items.title: CCI approves stake purchase in Burger King India operator by Lenexis consortium
items.summary: The Competition Commission of India has given its approval for a partial ownership acquisition in Restaurant Brands Asia Limited. This deal involves a consortium led by Lenexis Foodworks Private Limited. The acquisition will occur through various steps including equity and warrant subscriptions, and an open offer. Restaurant Brands Asia operates Burger King outlets across India.

items.title: Navi Mumbai airport can hike passenger fees by 6% from next April, regulator says
items.summary: At  ₹620 for departing domestic passengers and  ₹270 for those arriving, Navi Mumbai airport already has the second-highest user development fees of any major Indian airport.

items.title: NCDEX introduces RAINMUMBAI weather future contracts
items.summary: The monthly futures contract is aimed at farmers, construction and power utility companies

items.title: No Ebola case in India yet, centre reviews preparedness after WHO declares global health emergency
items.summary: India's Health Ministry held a high-level meeting to assess preparedness for Ebola Virus Disease following a WHO declaration. Officials confirmed no cases in India and urged calm, emphasizing the nation's vigilance and readiness. States have been directed to ensure readiness at all levels, with SOPs circulated for screening, quarantine, and case management.

items.title: Ahead of Market: 10 things that will decide stock market action on Thursday
items.summary: Benchmark indices closed marginally higher on Wednesday, led by gains in banking and auto shares, while IT and FMCG stocks capped advances. Analysts said Nifty remains range-bound with weak sentiment persisting below key resistance levels, despite easing volatility and selective buying interest across sectors.

items.title: Meta layoff: Here is what 8,000 employees let go will receive as severance package
items.summary: Meta has begun laying off 8,000 employees globally as part of its restructuring to focus on AI. Affected workers will receive severance packages, with US employees getting 16 weeks of pay and healthcare coverage. This round of layoffs represents nearly 10% of Meta's workforce.

items.title: RBI reworks draft loan recovery directions following industry feedback
items.summary: The draft norms also bar lenders from disabling essential features such as internet access, incoming calls, emergency SOS services and public safety notifications

Milma milk price hike: Kerala cooperative raises rates by Rs 4 per litre from June 1
items.summary: Kerala milk cooperative Milma has announced a Rs 4 per litre milk price hike effective June 1, citing rising fuel, transportation and production costs. The cooperative said most of the increase will directly benefit dairy farmers across the state.

items.title: BHIM Payments App sees 300% growth in FY26 driven by cashbacks and user trust
items.summary: BHIM Payments App saw a significant 300% growth in FY26. Monthly transactions surged to 21.6 crore in March 2026. Cashback offers are a key growth driver. The app aims to expand to feature phones. NBSL is partnering with banks to embed BHIM technology into their mobile applications. This move will deepen the digital payment ecosystem across India.

items.title: Ola Electric misses FY26 revenue guidance, cuts losses in reset year
items.summary: On Wednesday, the Bengaluru-based company posted a 50% year-on-year decline in FY26 revenue at  ₹2,253 crore, well below its  ₹3,000-3,200 crore guidance issued in November. Ola Electric attributed the miss to an operational reset and higher investments in in-house cell manufacturing

items.title: Jubilant FoodWorks sees near-term margin hit as costs rise
items.summary: Jubilant FoodWorks faced multiple inflationary headwinds, particularly from LPG and PNG prices, wage inflation and rising logistics-linked commodity costs.

items.title: Gold, silver rates today: Comex gold gains $29/oz; silver jumps $1.8 as bond yields slip
items.summary: Precious metals found support as bond yields eased, with gold gaining $29 to $4,540 and silver rising $1.8 to $76.99. Despite mild gains, they remain affected by high Treasury yields and a strong dollar amid ongoing US-Iran tensions.

items.title: Adani, Reliance, Lodha, JSW bid for 206-acre Mumbai redevelopment projects
items.summary: Leading real estate firms are vying to redevelop three large housing colonies in Mumbai. These projects span prime areas like Worli, Bandra Reclamation, and Andheri West. The Maharashtra Housing and Area Development Authority is overseeing the process. This initiative aims to upgrade living standards for residents in older colonies. It promises modern housing and improved urban infrastructure for thousands.

items.title: New financial services features on app to help work in collaboration with banks, says NPCI BHIM Services MD
items.summary: BHIM Payments App’s total transaction volumes increased by 301 per cent in FY26 compared to FY25

items.title: Bombay House tightens grip on JLR as Tata Motors seeks to restore its crown jewel
items.summary: Tata Motors has cut JLR’s operating board from 13 members to three, and given CEO P.B. Balaji a £1.7-billion mandate to restore the British luxury carmaker as a more predictable engine of profit and cash flow

items.title: Bosch Ltd Q4FY26 net profit rises 3% to ₹569 crore on strong auto demand
items.summary: Revenue from operations up 13.3% at ₹5,566 crore in the quarter under review

items.title: RBI to infuse liquidity via $5 billion dollar rupee swap auction on May 26
items.summary: The RBI will conduct a $5 billion USD/INR buy-sell swap auction on May 26 to inject long-term liquidity into the banking system and strengthen forex reserves. The move aims to stabilise rupee volatility amid global pressures and recent currency depreciation linked to geopolitical tensions and oil price shocks.

items.title: Market Trading Guide: Buy Manappuram Finance and Apollo Micro Systems on Thursday for gains up to 8%
items.summary: Nifty sentiment is weak, consolidating between 23,400-23,800. The guide recommends buying Manappuram Finance (Target Rs 345) and Apollo Micro Systems (Target Rs 385) for near-term 8% gains. Both stocks showed strong bullish technical breakouts above resistance levels with volume support.

items.summary: Regulator suggests allowing salary deductions for MF investments, commission payouts in MF units and donations through schemes with safeguards

items.title: Govt meets industry to accelerate rollout of electric buses and trucks
items.summary: The Centre met electric bus and truck makers, fleet operators and banks to address financing and infrastructure hurdles in commercial EV adoption. India is considering credit guarantees and interest subsidies as it pushes cleaner transport and lower oil dependence.

items.title: SEBI’s revised savings formula lifts India’s FY25 savings ratio by 47 basis points
items.summary: Household savings through securities markets increases by ₹1.48 lakh crore after inclusion of secondary markets, REITs and private placements

items.title: Households pull Rs 54,786 cr worth of equities from secondary markets in FY25; invest record Rs 5.43 lakh crore in mutual funds
items.summary: Indian households demonstrated a structural shift in FY25. They pulled Rs 54,786 cr from secondary equities but doubled primary investments. A record Rs 5.43 lakh crore went into mutual funds, nearly doubling total securities market savings to Rs 6.91 lakh crore, favoring financial assets.

items.title: No change in user development fees at Navi Mumbai airport for FY27
items.summary: AERA’s decision to maintain these rates is aimed at easing the financial burden on travellers

items.title: Surat FPO turns cow-dung based bio-inputs into natural farming business
items.summary: The PBM Surat SPNF Producer Company has quietly built a ₹2-crore business around ‘Ghanjeevamrut’, a microbial-rich bio-input increasingly being adopted by farmers shifting away from chemical fertilisers

items.title: NCDEX launches derivative on Mumbai monsoon
items.summary: The derivative, RAINMUMBAI, has been launched in partnership with the India Meteorological Department and the Indian Institute of Technology, Bombay

items.title: Private, govt banks pledge full support to ECLGS 5.0
items.summary: Banks are fully supporting the new Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme 5.0. This scheme aims to help businesses facing challenges due to the West Asia conflict. Government officials highlighted its importance for MSMEs in Maharashtra. The scheme provides working capital support. A digital portal simplifies loan applications. This initiative will accelerate credit delivery to eligible businesses.

items.title: India's peak power demand hits fresh high of 265.44 GW on 20 May as heatwave persists
items.summary: This is the third consecutive day of record power demand in the country, after 257.37 GW on Monday and 260.45 GW on Tuesday.

items.summary: The meeting comes as the government weighs a proposed $1-billion incentive package to spur private-sector adoption of heavy commercial EVs.

items.title: Streaming platforms double down on South Indian hits
items.summary: South Indian cinema is increasingly dominating the streaming landscape, accounting for nearly 60% of theatrically successful films acquired by platforms in 2025. Stronger viewer engagement, evidenced by high completion rates, drives this trend, with regional content offering consistent value and a dependable acquisition ecosystem for streamers.

items.title: SMR Jewels ₹67 crore IPO opens May 26; price band set at ₹128–₹135 per share
items.summary: The IPO comprises a fresh issue of up to ₹54 crore and an offer for sale (OFS) of up to ₹13.23 crore, taking the total issue size to 49,80,000 shares

items.title: Milma to hike milk price by ₹4 per litre from June 1
items.summary: A mix of droughts, rising costs for milk from neighboring States, and skyrocketing transportation expenses due to global issues are driving the price rise

items.summary: West Asia conflict and US tariffs slowed leather export momentum during FY26

items.summary: The proposal marks a departure from current regulations that require that all mutual fund transactions must happen with the investor’s verified bank accounts, to enable maintenance of a digital trail.

items.title: NCDEX launches India’s first rainfall-based weather derivatives contract
items.summary: NCDEX has launched RAINMUMBAI, India’s first SEBI-approved exchange-traded weather derivatives contract based on Mumbai rainfall. Built with IIT Bombay and IMD data, it enables stakeholders like farmers, utilities, and lenders to hedge monsoon risks using a transparent, data-driven financial instrument.

items.title: Banks step up hiring of apprentices to cut costs, expand operations
items.summary: Data on recent recruitment trends show that banks are increasingly using apprentices to meet manpower requirements arising from the expansion of marketing, customer outreach, etc.

items.title: HR has no value in startups: Bolt CEO Ryan Breslow explains why, ‘When I let them go…’
items.summary: Ryan Breslow abandoned progressive policies to revive Bolt, eliminating four-day workweeks and unlimited PTO. After a severe valuation drop, he returned as CEO.

items.title: Kumaraswamy chairs consultation meeting to speed up adoption of e-buses & e-trucks
items.summary: Union Minister HD Kumaraswamy convened a high-level consultation in New Delhi to accelerate the adoption of electric buses and trucks nationwide. The meeting, involving fleet operators, financial institutions, and industry representatives, aimed to understand operational realities and strengthen government-industry collaboration for a sustainable clean mobility transition.

items.title: AI layoffs: Artificial Intelligence will destroy certain jobs, says HSBC CEO, urges staff to ‘not resist the change’
items.summary: Amid AI job cuts, HSBC's CEO Georges Elhedery encouraged staff to adapt and not resist technological changes. The bank may cut 20,000 roles, focusing on non-client-facing positions, while AI advancements are reshaping the workforce across the banking sector.

items.summary: The company on Wednesday inaugurated its global Technology and Innovation Centre in Tumakuru, Karnataka, positioning India as a key pillar in its global research and development network

items.title: 'Sweetening relationships since 1983': Parle reacts to Modi gifting Melody to Meloni
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi gifted Melody toffees to Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni. This gesture sparked a viral "Melodi" meme online. Parle Products shared the moment on social media. The company anticipates increased domestic and international sales following this global exposure. The act promoted Indian products on an international platform.

items.title: Rupee sinks to fresh record low amid dollar outflows and crude surge
items.summary: The rupee fell to a new closing low of 96.83 against the dollar as persistent foreign institutional investor outflows, rising crude oil prices, and global risk aversion weighed heavily on sentiment. Market experts warn of further weakness amid geopolitical tensions and continued capital flight.

items.title: Dr Reddy’s launches oral semaglutide biosimilar Obeda
items.summary: The tablets are available in 3 mg, 7 mg and 14 mg strengths and recommended for once-daily oral use

items.title: Stellantis plans partnerships with Dongfeng in Europe, JLR in US
items.summary: Stellantis is joining forces with China's Dongfeng to create a dynamic joint venture aimed at producing new energy vehicles right in the heart of Europe. With plans for manufacturing facilities in France, this partnership reflects Stellantis's bold strategy to widen its market influence.

items.title: Ola Electric cuts losses, but revenue plunges 56% in Q4FY26
items.summary: Revenue from operations fell 56% year-on-year to ₹265 crore in Q4FY26, reflecting lower vehicle deliveries and weak sales momentum during the quarter

items.title: LPG: Ship scarcity hits U.S. imports
items.summary: In March, 4.5 lakh LPG shipments left the U.S. for India, up from 3.2 lakh and 2.7 lakh tonnes in previous months, as per data from Kpler; subsequent months saw shipments drop to 3.72 lakh tonnes and 1.67 lakh tonnes due to less carrier availability

items.title: Wagh Bakri warns of sharp hit to West Asia exports, delays tea shipments by 45 days
items.summary: Wagh Bakri exports nearly 2.75 lakh kilograms of tea annually to Middle Eastern markets including the UAE, Kuwait, Oman, Qatar, Bahrain and Saudi Arabia

items.title: Dr Reddy’s launches oral semaglutide in India
items.summary: Dr Reddys Laboratories has launched its oral semaglutide biosimilar for type 2 diabetes in India. The drug, Obeda, is available in three tablet strengths for daily use. This launch expands access to advanced diabetes care. Dr Reddys is the second Indian company to introduce oral semaglutide domestically. The company is committed to reliable supply and consistent quality for Indian patients.

items.title: Vodafone Idea to invest Rs 4.33 crore for 26% stake in renewable energy SPV
items.summary: Vodafone Idea Limited has approved an investment of Rs 4.33 crore to acquire at least 26 per cent stake in MTK Quantum Green Energy Pvt. Ltd., marking a strategic push into captive renewable energy procurement through a solar-focused special purpose vehicle in Tamil Nadu.

items.title: RIL tells SC KG Basin arbitration was international dispute, not domestic
items.summary: Reliance Industries had not pursued the arbitration alone but had acted only as the operator under the production sharing contract on behalf of the entire consortium comprising it, BP and Niko, argues RIL lawyer.
